Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Gujarati (code=gu, script=Gujarati). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Bhavnagar.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Gujarati script for Gujarati (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 28
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Gujarat
- district: Bhavnagar
- first_language: Gujarati
- clinical_persona_summary: Rababen Koli is a 28-year-old female who is never married and resides in rural Bhavnagar, Gujarat, with primary education. She is a homemaker and primary language is Gujarati. Her lifestyle includes managing household finances and engaging in community duties.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhavnagar District Hospital]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0915-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|રબારીબેન કોળી]] [[AGE|28]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|03]]
આમ્બ્યુલન્સ દ્વારા આવ્યા [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|GJ14AB1234]] (બરાબર એક વાર).
સંબંધિત [[RELATIVE_NAME|જેઠાભાઈ કોળી]] ફોન [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] ડોક્ટર [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]
Vitals / acuity: દર્દી સભાન, સજાગ, મધ્યમ શ્વાસની તકલીફમાં. રૂમ એર પર SpO2 88%, શ્વાસ 28 શ્વાસ/મિનિટ. તાપમાન 102.4°F. HR 110 bpm, BP 140/90 mmHg. 3 દિવસથી કફ સાથે લોહીની ઉલટીની ફરિયાદ, તાવ અને વજન ઘટવાની સાથે.

Nursing Notes:
દર્દી જણાવે છે કે 3 દિવસથી ઉધરસ વધી રહી છે, શરૂઆતમાં સૂકી હતી, હવે ઓછી લોહીવાળા કફ સાથે. છાતીમાં દુખાવાનો ઇનકાર કરે છે પરંતુ ઊંડા શ્વાસ લેતી વખતે છાતીમાં દુખાવાની ફરિયાદ કરે છે. ઓછી તાવ, રાત્રે પરસેવો, અને ભૂખ ન લાગવાની ફરિયાદ. જણાવે છે કે છેલ્લા એક મહિનામાં વધુને વધુ થાક લાગે છે. TB નો કોઈ જાણીતો ઇતિહાસ નથી. કોઈ જાણીતી બીમારી નથી. 2 મહિના પહેલા સામાન્ય નબળાઈ માટે સ્થાનિક હેલ્થ કેમ્પમાં જોયા હતા. દર્દી નજીકના ગામની ગૃહિણી છે. પ્રારંભિક મૂલ્યાંકન સક્રિય પલ્મોનરી ટ્યુબરક્યુલોસિસની ઉચ્ચ સંભાવના સૂચવે છે શક્ય જટિલતાઓ સાથે. CBC, ESR, અને AFB smear અને culture માટે કફના નમૂના લેવામાં આવ્યા. તાત્કાલિક ચેસ્ટ X-ray ઓર્ડર કર્યો. દર્દીને ડ્રોપલેટ સાવચેતી પર રાખવામાં આવ્યા. SpO2 >92% જાળવવા માટે નેઝલ કેન્યુલા દ્વારા 2L/min પર ઓક્સિજન થેરાપી શરૂ કરી. ડાબા હાથમાં IV એક્સેસ સ્થાપિત કર્યો. દર્દીને સારવારનો સંપૂર્ણ કોર્સ પૂર્ણ કરવાના મહત્વ અને ફોલો-અપ વિશે શિક્ષિત કર્યા. પરિવારને કામચલાઉ નિદાન અને યોજના વિશે જાણ કરી.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]

  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]

  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]

  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]

  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Hindi (code=hi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jharkhand, district=Sahibganj.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Hindi (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 23
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Juice Clarification Supervisor
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jharkhand
- district: Sahibganj
- first_language: Hindi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 23-year-old female juice clarification supervisor from rural Jharkhand, never married, with an illiterate education level, speaks Hindi, and resides in Sahibganj district. She works methodically in a production setting, exhibits anxiety, and finds solace in folk music, yoga, and occasional mango consumption. Her lifestyle includes short local trips for shopping and occasional pilgrimages, with a preference for oral communication over written.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
--- सत्र ---
रोगी [[PATIENT_NAME|Sushila Murmu]] [[AGE|23]] [[GENDER|Female]]
अपॉइंटमेंट [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240815-001]] पोर्टल [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
क्लाइंट IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] डिवाइस IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] फोन [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
ईमेल [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|sushila.murmu@example.com]] अस्पताल [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sahibganj District Hospital]]
--- चैट ---
रोगी: नमस्ते डॉक्टर। मैं [[PATIENT_NAME|Sushila Murmu]] हूँ। मेरी उम्र 23 साल है और यह मेरी पहली गर्भावस्था है। पिछले दो दिनों से मुझे बहुत कमजोरी और चक्कर आ रहे हैं। मुझे पेट के निचले हिस्से में भी दर्द है। मैं चिंतित हूँ।
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: नमस्ते सुशीला। मैं आपकी चिंता समझता हूँ। क्या आप दर्द के बारे में और बता सकती हैं? क्या यह तेज़ है या हल्का? और 1 से 10 के पैमाने पर यह कितना गंभीर है?
रोगी: यह हल्का दर्द है, बहुत तेज़ नहीं। इसकी गंभीरता शायद 10 में से 4 है। यह आता-जाता रहता है। मुझे हर समय बहुत थकान महसूस होती है। मैं ठीक से खाने में सक्षम नहीं हुई हूँ।
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: समझाने के लिए धन्यवाद। गर्भावस्था के शुरुआती दौर में चक्कर आना और थकान होना सामान्य है, लेकिन हमें उन्हें नज़रअंदाज़ नहीं करना चाहिए। क्या आपने किसी रक्तस्राव या स्पॉटिंग पर ध्यान दिया है?
रोगी: नहीं, कोई रक्तस्राव नहीं है। केवल यह दर्द और चक्कर आना है। मुझे सुबह के समय पेट में थोड़ा बीमार महसूस होता है।
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: यह भी बहुत सामान्य है, हम इसे 'मॉर्निंग सिकनेस' कहते हैं। हालांकि, आपके लक्षणों को देखते हुए, मैं यह सुनिश्चित करना चाहता हूँ कि सब कुछ ठीक से आगे बढ़ रहा है। मैं आपके लिए [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sahibganj District Hospital]] में एक अल्ट्रासाउंड की व्यवस्था कर रहा हूँ। कृपया कल सुबह आएं।
रोगी: ठीक है डॉक्टर। मुझे किस समय आना चाहिए? और क्या मुझे टेस्ट से पहले कुछ खास करना होगा?
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: कृपया सुबह 9 बजे आएं। आप आने से पहले हल्का भोजन कर सकती हैं। इसके अलावा, यदि आपके पास आपका [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] कार्ड है, तो कृपया उसे साथ लाएं। इससे हमें आपके रिकॉर्ड तक तेज़ी से पहुँचने में मदद मिलेगी।
रोगी: मेरे पास ABHA कार्ड नहीं है। मैं बस अपने ID के साथ आऊँगी। क्या यह ठीक है?

Patient: Namaste doctor. I am [[PATIENT_NAME|Sushila Murmu]]. I am 23 years old and this is my first pregnancy. I am feeling very weak and dizzy since the last two days. I also have some pain in my lower abdomen. I am worried.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: Namaste Sushila. I understand your concern. Can you tell me more about the pain? Is it sharp or dull? And how severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
Patient: It is a dull pain, not very sharp. The severity is maybe 4 out of 10. It comes and goes. I feel very tired all the time. I have not been able to eat properly.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: Thank you for explaining. Dizziness and fatigue are common in early pregnancy, but we should not ignore them. Have you noticed any bleeding or spotting?
Patient: No, there is no bleeding. Only this pain and dizziness. I am also feeling a bit sick to my stomach in the mornings.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: That is also very common, we call it morning sickness. However, given your symptoms, I want to be sure everything is progressing well. I am arranging an ultrasound for you at the [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sahibganj District Hospital]]. Please come tomorrow morning.
Patient: Okay doctor. What time should I come? And do I need to do anything special before the test?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: Please come at 9 AM. You can have a light meal before coming. Also, please bring your [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] card if you have it. It will help us access your records faster.

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: Yes, that is perfectly fine. Just bring any photo ID, like your voter ID or Aadhaar card. We will see you tomorrow at 9 AM. In the meantime, try to rest and drink plenty of water.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. I will be there.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Singh]]: You are welcome, Sushila. Take care.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Hindi (code=hi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jharkhand, district=Sahibganj.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Hindi (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 23
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Juice Clarification Supervisor
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jharkhand
- district: Sahibganj
- first_language: Hindi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 23-year-old female juice clarification supervisor from rural Jharkhand, never married, with an illiterate education level, speaks Hindi and is employed in a supervisory role. She resides in a joint household and follows a simple lifestyle, engaging in folk music, yoga, and occasional mango consumption. Her work involves methodical oversight of filtration processes, and she is known for troubleshooting on the production floor.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Ramanagara.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Kannada script for Kannada (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 55
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Vehicle, Window and Related Cleaners, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Karnataka
- district: Ramanagara
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 55-year-old female from Karnataka, Ramanagara, who is currently married, works as a vehicle cleaner, has no formal education, and speaks only Kannada. She lives in an urban area and manages her health conservatively while dealing with knee arthritis. Her lifestyle includes a conservative approach to spending and occasional use of traditional cleaning methods.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ಪ್ರಿಸ್ಕ್ರಿಪ್ಷನ್ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sri Sathya Sai Institute of Higher Medical Sciences, Ramanagara]]
ರೋಗಿ [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai]], [[AGE|55]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|RX-2024-0815-001]]
ಡಾ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]
ಫೋನ್: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9886543210]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
ರೋಗಿ ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-88776]]
ವಿಳಾಸ: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|15th Cross, Near KSRTC Bus Stand, Ramanagara Town, Karnataka - 562159]]
ಜಿಲ್ಲೆ: [[DISTRICT|Ramanagara]]

ರೋಗನಿರ್ಣಯ: ಮೂಳೆ ಮೆಟಾಸ್ಟಾಸಿಸ್‌ನೊಂದಿಗೆ ಮೆಟಾಸ್ಟಾಟಿಕ್ ಸ್ತನ ಕ್ಯಾನ್ಸರ್, ಎಡ ಮೊಣಕಾಲಿನ ದೀರ್ಘಕಾಲದ ಅಸ್ಥಿಸಂಧಿವಾತ.

Rx:
1.  ಪ್ಯಾಕ್ಲಿಟಾಕ್ಸೆಲ್ 175 mg/m² IV 3 ಗಂಟೆಗಳ ಕಾಲ ದಿನ 1, 8, 15, 22 ರಂದು 28-ದಿನಗಳ ಚಕ್ರದಲ್ಲಿ.
2.  ಡೆಕ್ಸಮೆಥಾಸೋನ್ 8 mg PO/IV ದಿನಕ್ಕೆ ಎರಡು ಬಾರಿ 3 ದಿನಗಳವರೆಗೆ ಪ್ಯಾಕ್ಲಿಟಾಕ್ಸೆಲ್ ನೀಡುವ ಹಿಂದಿನ ದಿನದಿಂದ ಪ್ರಾರಂಭಿಸಿ.
3.  ಜೋಲೆಡ್ರೊನಿಕ್ ಆಸಿಡ್ 4 mg IV ಕನಿಷ್ಠ 15 ನಿಮಿಷಗಳ ಕಾಲ ದಿನ 1 ರಂದು 28-ದಿನಗಳ ಚಕ್ರದಲ್ಲಿ.
4.  ಪ್ಯಾರಸಿಟಮಾಲ್ 500 mg PO QID PRN ನೋವಿಗೆ.
5.  ಐಬುಪ್ರೊಫೇನ್ 400 mg PO TID PRN ಸಂಧಿವಾತದ ನೋವಿಗೆ, ಆಹಾರದೊಂದಿಗೆ.

ಪ್ರತಿಕ್ರಿಯೆಯ ಮೌಲ್ಯಮಾಪನ ಮತ್ತು ಅಡ್ಡಪರಿಣಾಮಗಳ ನಿರ್ವಹಣೆಗಾಗಿ 3 ವಾರಗಳ ನಂತರ ಆಂಕೊಲಾಜಿ OPD ಯಲ್ಲಿ ಫಾಲೋ-ಅಪ್ ಮಾಡಿ.
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|savitri.bai@abdm]]

Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai]], [[AGE|55]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|RX-2024-0815-001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]

Address: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|15th Cross, Near KSRTC Bus Stand, Ramanagara Town, Karnataka - 562159]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Ramanagara]]

Diagnosis: Metastatic Breast Cancer with Bone Metastasis, Chronic Osteoarthritis of Left Knee.

Rx:
1.  Paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV over 3 hours on Day 1, 8, 15, 22 of a 28-day cycle.
2.  Dexamethasone 8 mg PO/IV twice daily for 3 days starting the day before Paclitaxel.
3.  Zoledronic Acid 4 mg IV over at least 15 minutes on Day 1 of a 28-day cycle.
4.  Paracetamol 500 mg PO QID PRN for pain.
5.  Ibuprofen 400 mg PO TID PRN for arthritis pain, with food.

Follow-up in Oncology OPD in 3 weeks for assessment of response and management of side effects.
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|savitri.bai@abdm]]
judge_reasoning: Kannada prose throughout narrative; oncology content fits 55yo female; all TYPEs allowed; Latin tags and drug names permitted; no script/dialect/domain issues.

professional_persona: Gangavva Mathapati is a seasoned vehicle and interior cleaner who combines meticulous dust‑removal techniques with traditional cleaning agents like soda ash and neem leaves, and they pragmatically experiment with simple new tools to boost efficiency while consistently delivering courteous service to clients.
linguistic_persona: Gangavva Mathapati speaks fluent spoken Kannada, skillfully using local idioms and gestures to convey instructions, and they pick up occasional Hindi words from neighbours while understanding basic work‑related English terms like 'vehicle' and 'cleaner'.
sports_persona: Gangavva Mathapati enjoys cheering for the Royal Challengers Bangalore during IPL matches, follows the Indian cricket team's games, and occasionally participates in community badminton sessions at the local Ramanagara club, staying modestly active while preferring low‑impact activities.
arts_persona: Gangavva Mathapati immerses themselves in Kannada folk music, especially songs by K.S. Natha and Hamsalekha, regularly watches classic Dr. Rajkumar films at the Ramanagara community hall, and they enjoy crafting simple beaded decorations inspired by local folk art.
travel_persona: Gangavva Mathapati prefers short, well‑planned trips to nearby destinations such as Nandi Hills, Mysore Palace, and the Tirumala shrine, balancing modest budgets with the desire to experience cultural festivals and family gatherings, and they often travel by shared auto or personal car with a small group of relatives.
culinary_persona: Gangavva Mathapati loves preparing simple traditional dishes such as ragi mudde and bisi bele bath, relishes the earthy flavor of ragi flour, tamarind, and fresh coriander, and they prefer home‑cooked meals over restaurant food, often using seasonal vegetables from their garden.
persona: Gangavva Mathapati is a diligent yet modest cleaning entrepreneur who mixes age‑old neem‑based tricks with occasional gadget cravings, manages money conservatively while secretly splurging on shiny tools, and quietly battles knee arthritis with daily turmeric tea.
cultural_background: Gangavva Mathapati belongs to the Kannada‑speaking community of Ramanagara, a town on the outskirts of Bengaluru known for its granite hills and silk‑cocoon markets. Growing up in an urban environment, she absorbed the everyday rhythms of South‑Indian life – the early‑morning temple bells, the aroma of fresh ragi mudde, and the vibrant celebrations of Ugadi, Dasara and Deepavali. Her family embraces traditional joint‑family values, where respect for elders and a sense of collective duty are paramount. This cultural backdrop shapes her practical outlook, balancing a curiosity for new cleaning tools (jugaad) with a deep reverence for time‑tested household practices.
linguistic_background: Gangavva's linguistic repertoire centres on spoken Kannada. Being illiterate, she cannot read or write Kannada or any other script, but she comfortably engages in daily conversations, understands local idioms, and follows oral instructions at work. She has no formal knowledge of a second language, though occasional Hindi phrases from neighbours seep into her speech. Her exposure to Indian English is limited to work‑related terms such as 'vehicle', 'window' and 'cleaner' that she picks up through listening.
skills_and_expertise: Gangavva is skilled in thorough vehicle interior cleaning, adept at removing dust, stains and odours from seats, carpets and dashboards. She efficiently cleans windows of cars, homes and commercial premises, using a mix of traditional cleaning agents like soda ash, neem leaves and modern supplies where available. Her expertise includes surface disinfection, careful handling of cleaning tools, time management for multiple jobs in a day, and courteous interaction with clients, even though she communicates mainly through gestures and simple spoken Kannada.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Vehicle interior cleaning', 'Window cleaning', 'Surface disinfection', 'Use of traditional cleaning agents', 'Time management', 'Client interaction']
hobbies_and_interests: In her leisure time, Gangavva enjoys listening to Kannada folk songs on the radio, visiting the local temple during festivals, and tending to a small garden of seasonal vegetables and herbs. She loves preparing simple traditional dishes such as ragi mudde and bisi bele bath for family gatherings. Occasionally she engages in hand‑craft activities like beading or making simple decorative items, and she likes watching regional movies at the community hall.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Kannada folk songs', 'Temple visits during festivals', 'Gardening seasonal vegetables and herbs', 'Cooking traditional Kannada dishes', 'Hand‑crafting simple decorative items', 'Watching regional movies']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Gangavva aims to secure a steady flow of cleaning contracts in Ramanagara, hoping to become the trusted go‑to cleaner for local households and small businesses. She wishes to upgrade her tools with a modest motorised vacuum cleaner and to pick up a few efficient cleaning shortcuts (jugaad) from experienced peers. Over the next few years she plans to mentor younger women in her community, helping them earn a livelihood through similar work, while also building a comfortable financial safety net for her family’s future.
occupation: Vehicle, Window and Related Cleaners, Other
persona_clinical_summary: A 55-year-old female from Karnataka, Ramanagara, who is currently married and works as a vehicle cleaner in an urban setting. She is illiterate, speaks Kannada, and manages her finances conservatively while dealing with knee arthritis. Her lifestyle includes conservative spending with occasional small splurges on cleaning tools and a preference for home-cooked traditional meals.

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Lab Report for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Ramanagara.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Kannada script for Kannada (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 55
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Vehicle, Window and Related Cleaners, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Karnataka
- district: Ramanagara
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 55-year-old female from Karnataka, Ramanagara, who is currently married and works as a vehicle cleaner in an urban setting. She is illiterate, speaks Kannada, and manages her finances conservatively while dealing with knee arthritis. Her lifestyle includes conservative spending with occasional small splurges on cleaning tools and a preference for home-cooked traditional meals.

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] MRN [[MRN|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|41]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Results table (analyte values NOT tagged as PHI entities).

MANDATORY inline entities (10 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- STUDENT_ID — Student ID e.g. [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

[[PATIENT_NAME|ಸವಿತಾ ಬಾಯಿ]] [[AGE|55]] [[GENDER|ಮಹಿಳೆ]] ಆದೇಶಿಸಿದವರು [[DOCTOR_NAME|ಡಾ. ಅಂಜಲಿ ಶರ್ಮಾ]]

ರೋಗಿಯ ವಿವರಗಳು:
ರೋಗಿ ಐಡಿ: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
ಫೋನ್ ಸಂಖ್ಯೆ: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
ಜಿಲ್ಲೆ: [[DISTRICT|ರಾಮನಗರ]]
ವೃತ್ತಿ: ವಾಹನ, ಕಿಟಕಿ ಮತ್ತು ಸಂಬಂಧಿತ ಸ್ವಚ್ಛಗೊಳಿಸುವವರು, ಇತರ

ಪ್ರಯೋಗಾಲಯದ ಸಂಶೋಧನೆಗಳು:
ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣ ರಕ್ತ ಕಣಗಳ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆ (CBC):
- ಹಿಮೋಗ್ಲೋಬಿನ್: 11.2 g/dL
- ಬಿಳಿ ರಕ್ತ ಕಣಗಳ ಸಂಖ್ಯೆ: 7.5 x 10^9/L
- ಕೆಂಪು ರಕ್ತ ಕಣಗಳ ಸಂಖ್ಯೆ: 4.8 x 10^12/L
- ಪ್ಲೇಟ್‌ಲೆಟ್ ಸಂಖ್ಯೆ: 250 x 10^9/L

ಜೈವಿಕ ರಸಾಯನಶಾಸ್ತ್ರ:
- ಉಪವಾಸದ ರಕ್ತದ ಗ್ಲೂಕೋಸ್: 98 mg/dL
- ಒಟ್ಟು ಕೊಲೆಸ್ಟ್ರಾಲ್: 185 mg/dL
- HDL ಕೊಲೆಸ್ಟ್ರಾಲ್: 45 mg/dL
- LDL ಕೊಲೆಸ್ಟ್ರಾಲ್: 110 mg/dL
- ಟ್ರೈಗ್ಲಿಸರೈಡ್‌ಗಳು: 140 mg/dL
- ಸೀರಮ್ ಕ್ರಿಯೇಟಿನೈನ್: 0.9 mg/dL
- ಸೀರಮ್ ಸೋಡಿಯಂ: 138 mEq/L
- ಸೀರಮ್ ಪೊಟ್ಯಾಸಿಯಮ್: 4.1 mEq/L
- ಲಿವರ್ ಫಂಕ್ಷನ್ ಟೆಸ್ಟ್ (AST, ALT): ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಮಿತಿಯೊಳಗೆ

ಥೈರಾಯ್ಡ್ ಕಾರ್ಯ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಗಳು:
- TSH: 2.5 mIU/L
- ಫ್ರೀ T4: 1.2 ng/dL

ಮನೋವೈದ್ಯಕೀಯ ಮೌಲ್ಯಮಾಪನ ಸಾರಾಂಶ:
ರೋಗಿಯು ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಆತಂಕ ಮತ್ತು ಕಡಿಮೆ ಮನಸ್ಥಿತಿಯ ಲಕ್ಷಣಗಳೊಂದಿಗೆ ಕಾಣಿಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತಾರೆ, ಇದು ಖಿನ್ನತೆಯ ಸಂಚಿಕೆಗೆ ಅನುಗುಣವಾಗಿದೆ. ಮನೋವೈದ್ಯಕೀಯ ಪ್ರಸ್ತುತಿಯನ್ನು ವಿವರಿಸುವ ಯಾವುದೇ ಗಮನಾರ್ಹ ಚಯಾಪಚಯ ಅಸಹಜತೆಗಳು ಕಂಡುಬಂದಿಲ್ಲ. ಥೈರಾಯ್ಡ್ ಕಾರ್ಯವು ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಮಿತಿಯಲ್ಲಿದೆ. ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣ ರಕ್ತ ಕಣಗಳ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆ ಮತ್ತು ಮೆಟಬಾಲಿಕ್ ಪ್ಯಾನಲ್ ಗಮನಾರ್ಹವಾಗಿಲ್ಲ.

ವೈದ್ಯಕೀಯ ಪ್ರಭಾವ:
1. ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಆತಂಕದ ಅಸ್ವಸ್ಥತೆ, ಮಧ್ಯಮ ತೀವ್ರತೆ
2. ಪ್ರಮುಖ ಖಿನ್ನತೆಯ ಸಂಚಿಕೆ, ಸೌಮ್ಯ ತೀವ್ರತೆ
3. ಮೊಣಕಾಲು ಅಸ್ಥಿಸಂಧಿವಾತ (ವೈದ್ಯಕೀಯ ಸಂಬಂಧ)

ಯೋಜನೆ:
1. ಆತಂಕ ಮತ್ತು ಖಿನ್ನತೆಯ ಲಕ್ಷಣಗಳಿಗಾಗಿ ಕಡಿಮೆ ಪ್ರಮಾಣದ SSRI ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆಯನ್ನು ಪ್ರಾರಂಭಿಸಿ.
2. ಕಾಗ್ನಿಟಿವ್ ಬಿಹೇವಿಯರಲ್ ಥೆರಪಿಗಾಗಿ ಕೌನ್ಸೆಲಿಂಗ್ ಸೇವೆಗಳಿಗೆ ಶಿಫಾರಸು ಮಾಡಿ.
3. ಪ್ರತಿಕ್ರಿಯೆ ಮತ್ತು ಅಡ್ಡಪರಿಣಾಮಗಳನ್ನು ಮೌಲ್ಯಮಾಪನ ಮಾಡಲು 4 ವಾರಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಫಾಲೋ-ಅಪ್ ಮಾಡಿ.
4. ಅಗತ್ಯವಿದ್ದಲ್ಲಿ NSAID ಗಳೊಂದಿಗೆ ಮೊಣಕಾಲು ಅಸ್ಥಿಸಂಧಿವಾತದ ನಿರ್ವಹಣೆಯನ್ನು ಮುಂದುವರಿಸಿ ಮತ್ತು ಫಿಸಿಯೋಥೆರಪಿ ಶಿಫಾರಸು.

ವರದಿಯನ್ನು 2024-08-15 ರಂದು ಡಾ. ಅಂಜಲಿ ಶರ್ಮಾ ಅವರು ಸಿದ್ಧಪಡಿಸಿದ್ದಾರೆ.

professional_persona: Gangavva Mathapati is a seasoned vehicle and interior cleaner who combines meticulous dust‑removal techniques with traditional cleaning agents like soda ash and neem leaves, and they pragmatically experiment with simple new tools to boost efficiency while consistently delivering courteous service to clients.
linguistic_persona: Gangavva Mathapati speaks fluent spoken Kannada, skillfully using local idioms and gestures to convey instructions, and they pick up occasional Hindi words from neighbours while understanding basic work‑related English terms like 'vehicle' and 'cleaner'.
sports_persona: Gangavva Mathapati enjoys cheering for the Royal Challengers Bangalore during IPL matches, follows the Indian cricket team's games, and occasionally participates in community badminton sessions at the local Ramanagara club, staying modestly active while preferring low‑impact activities.
arts_persona: Gangavva Mathapati immerses themselves in Kannada folk music, especially songs by K.S. Natha and Hamsalekha, regularly watches classic Dr. Rajkumar films at the Ramanagara community hall, and they enjoy crafting simple beaded decorations inspired by local folk art.
travel_persona: Gangavva Mathapati prefers short, well‑planned trips to nearby destinations such as Nandi Hills, Mysore Palace, and the Tirumala shrine, balancing modest budgets with the desire to experience cultural festivals and family gatherings, and they often travel by shared auto or personal car with a small group of relatives.
culinary_persona: Gangavva Mathapati loves preparing simple traditional dishes such as ragi mudde and bisi bele bath, relishes the earthy flavor of ragi flour, tamarind, and fresh coriander, and they prefer home‑cooked meals over restaurant food, often using seasonal vegetables from their garden.
persona: Gangavva Mathapati is a diligent yet modest cleaning entrepreneur who mixes age‑old neem‑based tricks with occasional gadget cravings, manages money conservatively while secretly splurging on shiny tools, and quietly battles knee arthritis with daily turmeric tea.
cultural_background: Gangavva Mathapati belongs to the Kannada‑speaking community of Ramanagara, a town on the outskirts of Bengaluru known for its granite hills and silk‑cocoon markets. Growing up in an urban environment, she absorbed the everyday rhythms of South‑Indian life – the early‑morning temple bells, the aroma of fresh ragi mudde, and the vibrant celebrations of Ugadi, Dasara and Deepavali. Her family embraces traditional joint‑family values, where respect for elders and a sense of collective duty are paramount. This cultural backdrop shapes her practical outlook, balancing a curiosity for new cleaning tools (jugaad) with a deep reverence for time‑tested household practices.
linguistic_background: Gangavva's linguistic repertoire centres on spoken Kannada. Being illiterate, she cannot read or write Kannada or any other script, but she comfortably engages in daily conversations, understands local idioms, and follows oral instructions at work. She has no formal knowledge of a second language, though occasional Hindi phrases from neighbours seep into her speech. Her exposure to Indian English is limited to work‑related terms such as 'vehicle', 'window' and 'cleaner' that she picks up through listening.
skills_and_expertise: Gangavva is skilled in thorough vehicle interior cleaning, adept at removing dust, stains and odours from seats, carpets and dashboards. She efficiently cleans windows of cars, homes and commercial premises, using a mix of traditional cleaning agents like soda ash, neem leaves and modern supplies where available. Her expertise includes surface disinfection, careful handling of cleaning tools, time management for multiple jobs in a day, and courteous interaction with clients, even though she communicates mainly through gestures and simple spoken Kannada.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Vehicle interior cleaning', 'Window cleaning', 'Surface disinfection', 'Use of traditional cleaning agents', 'Time management', 'Client interaction']
hobbies_and_interests: In her leisure time, Gangavva enjoys listening to Kannada folk songs on the radio, visiting the local temple during festivals, and tending to a small garden of seasonal vegetables and herbs. She loves preparing simple traditional dishes such as ragi mudde and bisi bele bath for family gatherings. Occasionally she engages in hand‑craft activities like beading or making simple decorative items, and she likes watching regional movies at the community hall.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Kannada folk songs', 'Temple visits during festivals', 'Gardening seasonal vegetables and herbs', 'Cooking traditional Kannada dishes', 'Hand‑crafting simple decorative items', 'Watching regional movies']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Gangavva aims to secure a steady flow of cleaning contracts in Ramanagara, hoping to become the trusted go‑to cleaner for local households and small businesses. She wishes to upgrade her tools with a modest motorised vacuum cleaner and to pick up a few efficient cleaning shortcuts (jugaad) from experienced peers. Over the next few years she plans to mentor younger women in her community, helping them earn a livelihood through similar work, while also building a comfortable financial safety net for her family’s future.
occupation: Vehicle, Window and Related Cleaners, Other
persona_clinical_summary: A 55-year-old female from Karnataka, Ramanagara, who is currently married and works as a vehicle cleaner. She is illiterate, speaks Kannada, and lives in an urban setting. She manages knee arthritis and prefers conservative spending with occasional tool purchases.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Ramanagara.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Kannada script for Kannada (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 55
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Vehicle, Window and Related Cleaners, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Karnataka
- district: Ramanagara
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 55-year-old female from Karnataka, Ramanagara, who is currently married and works as a vehicle cleaner. She is illiterate, speaks Kannada, and lives in an urban setting. She manages knee arthritis and prefers conservative spending with occasional tool purchases.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ಫಲಾನುಭವಿ [[PATIENT_NAME|Lakshmi]], [[AGE|55]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ಆಶಾ: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Shanthamma]] | ಫೋನ್ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
ಭೇಟಿಯ ಸಂಶೋಧನೆಗಳು: ರೋಗಿಯು ಕಳೆದ ಒಂದು ವಾರದಿಂದ ಮೊಣಕಾಲು ನೋವಿನ ಹೆಚ್ಚಳದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ವರದಿ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದಾರೆ, ಮಾರುಕಟ್ಟೆಗೆ ನಡೆಯಲು ಕಷ್ಟವಾಗುತ್ತಿದೆ. ಅವರು ಪ್ಯಾರಸಿಟಮಾಲ್ ತೆಗೆದುಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾರೆ ಆದರೆ ಅಲ್ಪ ಪ್ರಮಾಣದ ಉಪಶಮನ ಮಾತ್ರ ಸಿಕ್ಕಿದೆ. ಜ್ವರ ಅಥವಾ ಊತ ಕಂಡುಬಂದಿಲ್ಲ. ವಿಶ್ರಾಂತಿ ಪಡೆಯಲು ಮತ್ತು ಬೆಚ್ಚಗಿನ ಶಾಖವನ್ನು ಹಚ್ಚಲು ಸಲಹೆ ನೀಡಲಾಗಿದೆ. ಮೂರು ದಿನಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಪುನಃ ಸಂಪರ್ಕಿಸಲಾಗುವುದು.

[[RELIGION|Hindu]]

professional_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal, a 26‑year‑old bus driver in Uttara Kannada, blends meticulous route‑planning and disciplined vehicle maintenance with a sociable, team‑oriented demeanor, ensuring punctual and safe journeys for his passengers.
linguistic_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal fluently speaks Konkani and Kannada using the Kannada script, and can read and write English in the Latin script, allowing them to handle village paperwork, passenger tickets, and road signs with ease.
sports_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal regularly joins village cricket matches, cheering for the Karnataka state team and the local Karwar Cricket Club, and maintains fitness through quick sprints between bus stops, though they admit to skipping formal gym sessions.
arts_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal enjoys the rhythmic beats of Konkani folk songs by singers like Laxman Goud, assists backstage in Yakshagana performances, and occasionally watches Bollywood classics starring Madhuri Dixit and Amitabh Bachchan for inspiration.
travel_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal prefers off‑duty road trips to nearby towns like Mangalore and Goa, planning itineraries on the fly while keeping costs low, and dreams of exploring the backwaters of Kerala during a future holiday break.
culinary_persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal loves coastal delicacies such as bangda (mackerel) curry, neer dosa with coconut chutney, and sol kadhi, often savoring the tangy taste of kokum and tamarind, and can whip up a simple fish fry with mustard seeds and curry leaves when at home.
persona: Basavaraj Gorabaal is a disciplined bus driver who balances precise route‑planning with lively Konkani bhajan humming, but has a habit of obsessively checking the bus’s fuel gauge and occasionally over‑engineering fixes with duct‑tape.
cultural_background: Basavaraj hails from a rural coastal community in Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, where Konkani culture blends with the broader Kannada milieu. His family follows traditional Hindu customs, celebrates coastal festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Ugadi, and participates in folk arts such as Yakshagana and Konkani bhajans. The daily rhythm revolves around agriculture, fishing, and the bus routes that connect villages to towns, reflecting a pragmatic, community‑oriented way of life.
linguistic_background: Basavaraj is fluent in Konkani, his mother tongue, and uses the Kannada script for reading and writing it. He is also fully proficient in Kannada, the state language, both in spoken and written form, which enables him to communicate with locals and manage official paperwork. His education up to Higher Secondary gave him functional proficiency in English, allowing him to understand road signs, fill out transport logs, and interact with passengers from diverse backgrounds. Overall, he reads and writes comfortably in Konkani (Kannada script), Kannada (Kannada script), and English (Latin script).
skills_and_expertise: Basavaraj possesses solid bus‑driving expertise, including safe handling of a heavy passenger vehicle on varied rural and highway roads. He is skilled in route planning, punctual schedule adherence, and passenger management, ensuring a calm and orderly journey. His practical knowledge extends to routine vehicle maintenance, basic mechanical troubleshooting, and emergency response. He demonstrates strong interpersonal communication in Konkani, Kannada and English, and employs disciplined record‑keeping for logs, fares, and safety checks. His organized mindset aids in optimizing fuel efficiency and managing daily operational challenges.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Bus driving", "Vehicle maintenance", "Route planning", "Customer service", "Time management", "Interpersonal communication", "Multilingual communication (Konkani, Kannada, English)", "Basic mechanical troubleshooting"]
hobbies_and_interests: Basavaraj enjoys participating in village cricket matches and listening to Konkani folk songs that remind him of coastal celebrations. He takes part in Yakshagana performances during festivals, both as a backstage helper and occasional performer. In his free time, he likes tinkering with his bus engine using simple mutta (jugaad) solutions, reading devotional literature, and exploring nearby towns on off‑duty routes. Socialising with fellow drivers over tea, cooking traditional coastal dishes, and attending local fairs also feature prominently in his leisure.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Playing cricket (village team)", "Listening to Konkani folk music", "Participating in Yakshagana performances", "Vehicle tinkering (jugaad)", "Reading devotional literature", "Exploring nearby towns on off‑duty routes", "Socialising with fellow drivers over tea", "Cooking coastal dishes"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Basavaraj aims to upgrade his professional credentials by completing a certified Advanced Driver Training program and obtaining a Commercial Passenger Vehicle licence. He aspires to become a senior bus driver, responsible for training junior conductors and overseeing route safety. In the longer term, he wishes to manage a small fleet of intra‑district buses or open his own transport service, providing reliable connectivity for rural villages. His goals reflect his desire for structured advancement, higher earnings to support his family, and contributing to safer, more punctual public transport in his region.
persona_clinical_summary: A 26-year-old male bus driver from rural Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, who is never married and has completed higher secondary education. He is fluent in Konkani and Kannada, and works as a driver for a bus service. His lifestyle includes a habit of checking the bus's fuel gauge and a preference for off-duty road trips to nearby towns.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Konkani (code=kok, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Uttara Kannada.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Konkani (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 26
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Driver, Bus
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Uttara Kannada
- first_language: Konkani
- clinical_persona_summary: A 26-year-old male bus driver from rural Uttara Kannada, Karnataka, who is never married and has completed higher secondary education. He is fluent in Konkani and Kannada, and works as a driver for a bus service. His lifestyle includes a habit of checking the bus's fuel gauge and a preference for off-duty road trips to nearby towns.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

श्वसन: श्वासनली मध्यभागी आसा. छातीचो विस्तार समप्रमाणांत आसा. उजव्या सकयल्या भागांत ठोकर मारल्यार आवाज येना. स्वास घेताना आवाज सामान्य आसा, पूण उजव्या खांद्या सकयल्या भागांत खर आवाज (क्रॅकल्स) आयकू येतात. घरघर (व्हीझ) ना.

तपासणी:
छातीचो एक्स-रे (PA व्ह्यू): उजव्या सकयल्या भागांत हवा आनी द्रव भरिल्लो भाग दिसता, जो फुफ्फुसांत फोड (लंग ॲबसेस) आसपाचो संकेत दिता.
रक्त तपासणी: हिमोग्लोबिन: 13.5 g/dL, एकूण धव्या रक्तपेशी (WBC) संख्या: 12,500/cmm, न्यूट्रोफिलिया (85%) वांगडा.
ग्राम स्टेन आनी कल्चर खातीर कफ: प्रलंबित.

निदान:
1. उजव्या सकयल्या भागांत फुफ्फुसांत फोड
2. समाजांतल्यान जाल्लो न्युमोनिया

1. रुग्णाक शिरेतल्यान (IV) प्रतिजैविकां खातीर भरती करात. सुरवेक IV सेफ्ट्रिॲक्सोन 1g दिसाक एकदां आनी IV मेट्रोनिडाझोल 500mg दिसाक तीन फावटीं सुरू करात.
