Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

ऑपरेशन नोट: रुग्णाची माहिती: सुनिता पाटील, वय 35 वर्षे, स्त्री, ग्रामीण बेळगाव येथील सुरक्षा रक्षक, विवाहित, प्राथमिक शिक्षण पूर्ण. रुग्णाला उजव्या बाजूला 4 महिन्यांपासून वेदनादायक आणि न कमी होणारा हर्नियाचा त्रास होता, ज्यासोबत अधूनमधून मळमळ आणि उलट्या होत होत्या. तपासणीत उजव्या इंग्विनल भागात 6x5 सेमीचा वेदनादायक गाठ जाणवला, जो कमी करता आला नाही. रक्त तपासणी सामान्य मर्यादेत होती. रुग्णाला स्पाइनल ॲनेस्थेशिया देऊन उजव्या इंग्विनल हर्नियोप्लास्टीसाठी नेण्यात आले. उजव्या इंग्विनल भागात आडवा छेद देऊन सॅक ओळखला, कमी केला आणि हाय लिगेशन केले. नॉन-ॲबसॉर्बेबल सुचर्स वापरून बासिनी तंत्राने फ्लोअर दुरुस्त केला. जखम थरांमध्ये बंद केली. रुग्णाने प्रक्रिया चांगल्या प्रकारे सहन केली आणि पोस्ट-ऑपरेटिव्ह वॉर्डमध्ये हलवण्यात आले.

professional_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar is a vigilant rural security officer who excels at patrolling, crowd control and basic first aid, leveraging fluency in Marathi and Kannada to issue alerts, while they are curious about new safety tech and maintain a relaxed approach to paperwork that keeps them adaptable yet occasionally disorganized.
linguistic_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar speaks Marathi fluently and Kannada proficiently, can read simple notices in both languages, has a passive understanding of Hindi, and they are keen to improve literacy to handle more complex administrative tasks.
sports_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar avidly plays kabaddi with the village team, cheers for the Royal Challengers Bangalore in cricket, follows local football club FC Bengaluru United, and they organize spontaneous street cricket games during festivals, reflecting a competitive spirit and love for team camaraderie.
arts_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar enjoys listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs, sketches bustling village scenes on old notebook paper, admires street murals in nearby towns, and they follow the music of Lata Mangeshkar and the films of Rajkumar, letting these artistic influences fuel their creative side.
travel_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar prefers short road trips to nearby hill stations like Mahabaleshwar and historic towns like Hampi, plans travel with friends on a modest budget, enjoys spontaneous train rides during festivals, and they dream of visiting Goa's beaches when finances allow.
culinary_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar loves the tangy taste of Maharashtrian misal pav, the hearty texture of Karnataka's bisi bele bath, enjoys crunchy peanuts and spicy chivda at fairs, and they prefer homemade poha for breakfast, though they rarely cook themselves and often indulge in street food bazaars.
persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar blends vigilant rural security work with a love for folk music, spontaneous games, and modest savings, juggling curiosity and a laid‑back streak while dreaming of a supervisory role.
cultural_background: Ayyamma hails from the rural outskirts of Belgaum in Karnataka, a region where Marathi-speaking communities coexist with Kannada culture. Her family follows traditional agrarian customs, celebrates festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Ugadi, and maintains the strong communal bond typical of village life in the Deccan plateau.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Marathi, which she speaks fluently and understands well in both spoken and basic written form. She is also proficient in Kannada, allowing her to communicate effectively with neighbours and local authorities. Due to limited formal schooling (below primary), her literacy in Marathi and Kannada is functional but not advanced; she can read simple notices and fill out basic security logs. She has a passive understanding of Hindi but does not read or write it.
skills_and_expertise: Ayyamma has practical experience as a security officer in a rural setting. She is skilled in vigilant observation, patrolling, crowd control, and basic first aid. She can handle simple security equipment, maintain incident logs, and coordinate with local police when needed. Her multilingual ability allows her to communicate warnings and instructions in Marathi and Kannada effectively. Additionally, she possesses basic knowledge of village safety protocols and emergency response.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Vigilant observation', 'Patrolling', 'Crowd control', 'Basic first aid', 'Incident reporting', 'Multilingual communication (Marathi & Kannada)', 'Handling security equipment', 'Coordination with local police']
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys attending village fairs, listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs, and dancing during festivals. Ayyamma is curious about street art and loves sketching simple drawings of everyday scenes. She also participates in outdoor games like kabaddi and enjoys spontaneous group outings with friends. Occasionally, she watches Bollywood movies and reads comic strips for light entertainment.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Attending village fairs', 'Listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs', 'Dancing during festivals', 'Sketching everyday scenes', 'Playing kabaddi', 'Spontaneous group outings with friends', 'Watching Bollywood movies', 'Reading comic strips']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ayyamma aspires to become a senior security supervisor, seeking formal training and certification in safety management. She aims to improve her literacy in Marathi and Kannada to handle administrative duties more confidently. In the long term, she hopes to secure a stable position within a government security department, ensuring a steady income for her family while contributing to community safety.
persona_clinical_summary: A 35-year-old female security officer from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education. She speaks Marathi and Kannada, works in a rural security role, and resides in Belgaum district. Clinically, she has no reported chronic conditions but may have limited health literacy due to education level. Her lifestyle includes modest savings and a preference for local community activities.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Lab Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Belgaum.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Marathi (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 35
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Security Officer
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Belgaum
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 35-year-old female security officer from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education. She speaks Marathi and Kannada, works in a rural security role, and resides in Belgaum district. Clinically, she has no reported chronic conditions but may have limited health literacy due to education level. Her lifestyle includes modest savings and a preference for local community activities.

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] MRN [[MRN|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|41]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Results table (analyte values NOT tagged as PHI entities).

MANDATORY inline entities (10 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- STUDENT_ID — Student ID e.g. [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

She can handle simple security equipment, maintain incident logs, and coordinate with local police when needed. Her multilingual ability allows her to communicate warnings and instructions in Marathi and Kannada effectively. Additionally, she possesses basic knowledge of village safety protocols and emergency response.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]

  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Karnataka, district=Belgaum.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Marathi (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 35
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Security Officer
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Belgaum
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 35-year-old female security officer from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education. She speaks Marathi and Kannada, works in a rural security role, and resides in Belgaum district. Clinically, she has modest savings and a laid-back lifestyle, with no reported substance use or chronic conditions. Her health behaviors include occasional physical activity through kabaddi and a diet rich in traditional Maharashtrian and Karnataka foods.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ओपीडी स्लिप | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Belgaum]] | आयडी [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-BLG-001]]
रुग्ण: [[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Patil]] | जन्म तारीख [[DOB|1989-08-22]] | वय: [[AGE|35]] | लिंग: [[GENDER|Female]]
व्यवसाय: [[OCCUPATION|Security Officer]] | एमआरएन: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | डॉक्टर: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anita Deshmukh]]
नातेवाईक: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Patil]] | फोन: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
रजिस्ट्रार कर्मचारी आयडी: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | जिल्हा: [[DISTRICT|Belgaum]]
मुख्य तक्रार: गेल्या दोन महिन्यांपासून अनियमित मासिक पाळी आणि पोटात खालच्या बाजूला दुखणे.

सध्याच्या आजाराचा इतिहास:
रुग्ण, 35 वर्षांची महिला सुरक्षा रक्षक, ओलिगोमेनोरिया (कमी मासिक पाळी) या मुख्य तक्रारीसह आली आहे, ज्यामध्ये मासिक पाळी साधारणपणे दर 45-60 दिवसांनी येते. ती पोटात खालच्या बाजूला होणाऱ्या पेटके (cramping) बद्दल सांगते, जे मंद आणि सतत असतात, तीव्रतेमध्ये 4/10 आहेत. वेदना पसरत नाहीत आणि कोणत्याही विशिष्ट स्थितीमुळे त्या कमी होत नाहीत. तिला ताप, मळमळ, उलट्या किंवा शौचाच्या सवयींमध्ये बदल झाल्याचे नाकारते. तिला सौम्य पोट फुगल्यासारखे होणे आणि अधूनमधून स्तनांमध्ये कोमलता जाणवणे असे दिसून आले आहे. तिची शेवटची मासिक पाळी साधारणपणे 50 दिवसांपूर्वी होती, जी 5 दिवस टिकली आणि मध्यम स्वरूपाची होती. ती गेल्या एक वर्षापासून गर्भधारणेसाठी प्रयत्न करत आहे पण यश आले नाही.

प्रसूती इतिहास:
ग्रॅव्हिडा 2, पॅरा 1, अबॉर्शन 0, लिव्हिंग 1. तिला एक जिवंत मूल आहे, 7 वर्षांचा मुलगा. मागील प्रसूती बेलगाव येथील एका खाजगी रुग्णालयात सामान्य योनीमार्गाद्वारे झाली होती. गर्भधारणा किंवा प्रसूतीदरम्यान कोणतीही मोठी गुंतागुंत नव्हती.

स्त्रीरोग इतिहास:
तिने पूर्वी नियमित पॅप स्मीअर्स केले आहेत, शेवटचे दोन वर्षांपूर्वी केले होते, जे सामान्य असल्याचे नोंदवले गेले होते. तिला लैंगिक संक्रमित संसर्गाचे (STIs) निदान कधीही झालेले नाही. ती सध्या गर्भनिरोधकाचे कोणतेही साधन वापरत नाही.

भूतकाळातील वैद्यकीय इतिहास:
उच्च रक्तदाब, मधुमेह किंवा थायरॉईड विकारांसारख्या कोणत्याही जुनाट आजारांची माहिती नाही. शस्त्रक्रियेचा कोणताही इतिहास नाही.

कौटुंबिक इतिहास:
तिची आई जिवंत आहे आणि ठीक आहे, तिला कोणतीही स्त्रीरोगविषयक समस्या असल्याचे माहित नाही. तिच्या वडिलांना उच्च रक्तदाब आहे. तिला एक बहीण आहे, जी निरोगी आहे.

सामाजिक इतिहास:
तिचे लग्न झाले आहे आणि ती तिच्या पती आणि मुलासोबत बेलगावजवळील एका ग्रामीण गावात राहते. ती धूम्रपान करत नाही आणि मद्य किंवा तंबाखूचे कोणतेही सेवन करत नाही. तिच्या आहारात पारंपारिक महाराष्ट्रीयन आणि कर्नाटक खाद्यपदार्थांचा समावेश आहे. ती अधूनमधून शारीरिक हालचालींमध्ये गुंतलेली असते, ज्यामध्ये स्थानिक गटांसोबत कबड्डी खेळण्याचा समावेश आहे.

तपासणी:
सामान्य: रुग्ण सुदृढ, सुपोषण झालेली महिला, शुद्धीवर आणि जागरूक आहे.
पोट: मऊ, स्पर्शाने कोमल नाही, ओटीपोटाच्या भागात सौम्य कोमलता आहे. गार्डिंग किंवा रिबाउंड टेंडरनेस नाही. गर्भाशय अँटीव्हर्टेड, रेट्रोव्हर्टेड आणि सामान्य आकाराचे आहे, साधारणपणे 8 आठवड्यांच्या आकाराचे. कोणतेही ॲडनेक्सल मास जाणवत नाही.
स्पेक्युलम तपासणी: गर्भाशय ग्रीवा निरोगी आहे, ओएस बंद आहे. योनीच्या भिंती निरोगी आहेत.
बायमॅन्युअल तपासणी: गर्भाशय कोमल नाही, हलणारे आहे. गर्भाशय ग्रीवा घट्ट आहे. ॲडनेक्सा कोमल नाहीत आणि जाणवत नाहीत.

तपासण्या:
लघवी गर्भधारणा चाचणी: नकारात्मक.
हिमोग्लोबिन: 11.8 g/dL.
थायरॉईड स्टिम्युलेटिंग हार्मोन (TSH): 2.5 mIU/L.
फॉलिकल स्टिम्युलेटिंग हार्मोन (FSH): 12.5 mIU/mL.
ल्यूटिनायझिंग हार्मोन (LH): 18.0 mIU/mL.
प्रोजेस्टेरॉन (21 वा दिवस): 3.2 ng/mL.

निदान:
1. दुय्यम अ‍ॅमेनोरिया / ओलिगोमेनोरिया.
2. क्लिनिकल आणि बायोकेमिकल निष्कर्षांच्या आधारे संभाव्य पॉलीसिस्टिक ओव्हरी सिंड्रोम (PCOS).

योजना:
1. रुग्णाला तिच्या स्थितीबद्दल समुपदेशन केले, PCOS चे संभाव्य निदान आणि प्रजनन क्षमतेवर होणारे त्याचे परिणाम स्पष्ट केले.
2. वजन व्यवस्थापित करण्यासाठी आणि इन्सुलिन संवेदनशीलता सुधारण्यासाठी संतुलित आहार आणि नियमित व्यायामासह जीवनशैलीत बदल करण्याचा सल्ला दिला.
3. मासिक पाळी नियमित करण्यासाठी आणि ओव्हुलेशन सुधारण्यासाठी मेटफॉर्मिन 500 मिग्रॅ दिवसातून दोनदा लिहून दिले.
4. चक्र नियमित करण्यासाठी आणि एंडोमेट्रियल संरक्षण प्रदान करण्यासाठी कम्बाइंड ओरल कॉन्ट्रासेप्टिव्ह पिल (COC) लिहून दिले.
5. भविष्यात गर्भधारणेची इच्छा असल्यास पुढील मूल्यमापन आणि व्यवस्थापनासाठी तिला वंध्यत्व क्लिनिकमध्ये पाठवले.
6. लक्षणे आणि उपचारांना मिळणारा प्रतिसाद तपासण्यासाठी 3 महिन्यांत फॉलो-अप अपॉइंटमेंट शेड्यूल केली.
7. PCOS आणि निरोगी जीवनावर शैक्षणिक पत्रके प्रदान केली.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Dibrugarh.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Nepali (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 21
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Assam
- district: Dibrugarh
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 21-year-old female from Dibrugarh, Assam, who is married and has no formal occupation, reports limited education and speaks Nepali as her primary language. She maintains a traditional Nepali-Assamese cultural background and follows a Hindu faith. Her lifestyle includes modest financial management, daily multilingual communication, and a diet rich in traditional dishes like momos and sel roti.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

बिरामी [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Tamang]] [[AGE|21]] [[GENDER|Female]]
अपोइन्टमेन्ट [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] पोर्टल [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]

इमेल [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anjali.tamang@email.com]] अस्पताल [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Dibrugarh Civil Hospital]]
--- च्याट ---
बिरामी: नमस्कार डाक्टर साहेब, मेरो नाम अञ्जली तामाङ हो। मलाई पछिल्लो दुई दिनदेखि पेटको तल्लो दाहिने भागमा निकै दुखाइ भइरहेको छ। सुरुमा यो हल्का दुखाइ थियो तर अहिले यो तिखो र निरन्तर छ। मलाई ज्वरो पनि आएको छ र मलाई बान्ता भएको जस्तो महसुस भइरहेको छ।
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: नमस्कार अञ्जली, सम्पर्क गर्नुभएकोमा धन्यवाद। तपाईँलाई दुखाइ भइरहेको छ भन्ने कुरा मैले बुझेँ। के तपाईँ मलाई दुखाइको बारेमा थप बताउन सक्नुहुन्छ? हिँड्दा वा खोक्दा दुखाइ बढ्छ कि?
बिरामी: हो, डाक्टर। म हिँड्दा वा खोक्दा पनि निकै दुखाइ हुन्छ। मलाई धेरै कमजोरी महसुस भइरहेको छ र म केही पनि राम्ररी खान सकेको छैन।
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: मैले बुझेँ। तपाईँको लक्षणहरू जस्तै पेटको तल्लो दाहिने भागमा तिखो र स्थानीय दुखाइ, ज्वरो र वान्ताको आधारमा, मलाई एपेन्डिसाइटिस भनिने अवस्थाको चिन्ता छ। यो एक शल्यक्रियात्मक आपत्कालीन अवस्था हो। तपाईँले तुरुन्तै अस्पताल जानुपर्छ।
बिरामी: एपेन्डिसाइटिस? त्यहाँ के हुन्छ? के मलाई शल्यक्रिया आवश्यक पर्छ?
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: हो, सम्भवतः तपाईँलाई एपेन्डेक्स निकाल्नका लागि शल्यक्रिया आवश्यक पर्नेछ। यो एक धेरै सामान्य र सुरक्षित प्रक्रिया हो। हामीले स्क्यान गरेर निदान पुष्टि गर्नुपर्छ। कृपया तुरुन्तै दिब्रुगढ सिभिल अस्पतालको आपत्कालीन विभागमा जानुहोस्। मसित टेलिमेडिसिन परामर्श भएको जानकारी दिनुहोस्।
बिरामी: ठिक छ डाक्टर। म अहिले जान्छु। बाटोमा हुँदा मैले के गर्नुपर्छ?
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: कृपया केही पनि नखानुहोस् वा नपिउनुहोस्। शल्यक्रियाका लागि पेट खाली राख्नु महत्त्वपूर्ण छ। यदि तपाईँलाई उच्च ज्वरो छ भने तपाईँले प्यारासिटामोल लिन सक्नुहुन्छ, तर अन्य कुनै पनि दुखाइ कम गर्ने औषधिहरूबाट बच्नुहोस्। सावधानीपूर्वक गाडी चलाउनुहोस् वा कसैलाई तपाईँलाई लैजान भन्नुहोस्। हामी अस्पतालमा तपाईँको लागि तयार हुनेछौँ।
बिरामी: धेरै धेरै धन्यवाद डाक्टर। म हतार गर्छु।
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: तपाईँलाई स्वागत छ, अञ्जली। हामीले तपाईँको राम्रो हेरचाह गर्नेछौँ। कृपया आफ्नो फोन अन राख्नुहोस्।

Patient: Namaste doctor saheb, my name is Anjali Tamang. I am having severe pain in my lower right abdomen for the last two days. It started as a dull ache but now it is sharp and constant. I also have a fever and I feel like vomiting.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: Hello Anjali, thank you for reaching out. I understand you are in pain. Can you tell me more about the pain? Is it getting worse when you move or cough?
Patient: Yes, doctor. It hurts a lot when I walk or even when I cough. I feel very weak and I have not been able to eat anything properly.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: I see. Based on your symptoms of sharp, localized pain in the lower right abdomen with fever and nausea, I am concerned about a condition called appendicitis. This is a surgical emergency. You need to go to the hospital immediately.
Patient: Appendicitis? What will happen there? Do I need an operation?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: Yes, most likely you will need surgery to remove the appendix. It is a very common and safe procedure. We need to confirm the diagnosis with a scan. Please go to the emergency department of Dibrugarh Civil Hospital right away. Inform them you have a telemedicine consultation with me.
Patient: Okay doctor. I will go now. What should I do while I am on my way?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: Please do not eat or drink anything. It is important to keep your stomach empty for the surgery. You can take paracetamol if you have a high fever, but avoid any other painkillers. Drive carefully or ask someone to take you. We will be ready for you at the hospital.
Patient: Thank you so much, doctor. I will hurry.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Biren Sharma]]: You are welcome, Anjali. We will take good care of you. Please keep your phone on.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Dibrugarh.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Nepali (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 21
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Assam
- district: Dibrugarh
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 21-year-old female from Dibrugarh, Assam, who is currently married and has no formal occupation, reports limited education with only basic literacy. She is Nepali-speaking and resides in an urban zone, with a background that includes both Nepali and Assamese cultural practices. Her lifestyle is characterized by modest financial management and a habit of regularly checking radio news for updates.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Dibrugarh Civil Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Sharma]] [[AGE|21]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajiv Borah]]
Procedure / findings: Patient presented with a history of recurrent right lower quadrant abdominal pain for the past three months. Investigations including ultrasound and CT scan revealed a 6 cm appendiceal mass with surrounding phlegmon. The decision was made for an interval appendectomy. The patient was taken to the operating theatre and placed under general anesthesia. A standard McBurney incision was made. The appendix was found to be inflamed and adherent to the surrounding structures. Careful dissection was performed to free the appendix from the phlegmon. The base of the appendix was ligated with an absorbable suture and the appendix was removed. The stump was inverted and a purse-string suture was placed. The wound was irrigated with normal saline and closed in layers. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was shifted to the post-operative ward in stable condition.
[[BED_NUMBER|15]]
[[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|Sanjay Sharma]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-2024-0815-001]]

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Lab Report for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Dibrugarh.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Devanagari script for Nepali (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 21
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Assam
- district: Dibrugarh
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 21‑year‑old female from Dibrugarh, Assam, currently married, with an illiterate education level, works as a homemaker, and speaks Nepali as her first language. She resides in an urban zone and follows a Nepali-speaking Gorkha cultural background, celebrating festivals like Dashain and Tihar. Clinically, she has limited literacy, which may affect health literacy, and her multilingual communication in Hindi and Assamese could aid in patient education. Her lifestyle includes modest budgeting, traditional cooking, and community engagement, which may influence dietary habits and health behaviors.

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] MRN [[MRN|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|41]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Results table (analyte values NOT tagged as PHI entities).

MANDATORY inline entities (10 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- STUDENT_ID — Student ID e.g. [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

प्रयोगशालाको नतिजा:
नमुना सङ्कलन मिति: 15 August 2024
नमुनाको प्रकार: Blood
परीक्षणको नाम | सन्दर्भ दायरा | बिरामीको नतिजा
---|---|---
Hemoglobin (Hb) | 11.0 - 15.0 g/dL | 10.8 g/dL
Total Leukocyte Count (TLC) | 4000 - 11000 /µL | 8500 /µL
Platelet Count | 150000 - 450000 /µL | 210000 /µL
Blood Group | - | O Positive
Urine Routine | - | Normal
Blood Sugar (Fasting) | 70 - 100 mg/dL | 85 mg/dL
Urine Pregnancy Test | - | Positive

व्याख्या:
बिरामीको hemoglobin को स्तर थोरै कम छ, जसले हल्का anemia को सङ्केत गर्दछ। पूर्ण रक्त गणना र अन्य metabolic parameters सामान्य सीमा भित्र छन्। सकारात्मक urine pregnancy test ले pregnancy पुष्टि गर्दछ।

4. Prenatal care र पोषणमा counsel गर्नुहोस्।

Rita Sharma
LAB-AS-001
16 August 2024

user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Odia (code=or, script=Odia). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Odisha, district=Puri.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Odia script for Odia (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 20
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Odisha
- district: Puri
- first_language: Odia
- clinical_persona_summary: A 20-year-old female from Puri, Odisha, currently a homemaker with primary education, who speaks Odia as her first language and lives in a rural setting. She is never married and follows a lifestyle centered around traditional arts, community gatherings, and local cultural practices. Clinically, she may have limited exposure to formal healthcare systems due to her rural background and primary education.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

professional_persona: Sarsati Patra is a budding folk‑art entrepreneur who weaves handloom textiles and paints traditional Pattachitra, using their curiosity and community‑driven spirit to launch a modest home‑based studio that supports their family.
linguistic_persona: Sarsati Patra is fluent in Odia, manages basic Hindi gleaned from television and radio, and can spot common English words on their phone, though they struggle with extended conversation.
sports_persona: Sarsati Patra enjoys evening cricket matches with neighbourhood friends, follows the Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL, occasionally joins a women's kabaddi club at the village school, and prefers group sports that blend fitness with social camaraderie.
arts_persona: Sarsati Patra finds inspiration in the rhythmic chants of Odissi maestro Kelucharan Mohapatra, studies the vibrant Pattachitra canvases of Raghunath Behera, and unwinds to Bollywood hits featuring Madhuri Dixit, weaving these influences into their own sketches and woven designs.
travel_persona: Sarsati Patra loves spontaneous weekend getaways to the historic Konark Sun Temple and the sandy Puri shoreline, dreams of taking a pilgrimage to Varanasi’s ghats, and hopes to someday explore the vibrant streets of Bangkok and the serene beaches of Bali.
culinary_persona: Sarsati Patra excels at preparing classic Odia dishes such as dalma, pakhala bhata, chhena poda, and mustard‑spiced roti, relishes the tangy bite of sambhar with kokum, and often enjoys al‑fresco meals at the village beach stalls whenever possible.
persona: Sarsati Patra is a lively 20‑year‑old artisan who juggles temple sketches, kitchen experiments, and spontaneous beach walks, often losing track of time but always greeting friends with a bright smile.
cultural_background: Sarsati hails from the coastal rural belt of Puri in Odisha, a region steeped in Jagannath temple traditions, vibrant folk arts such as Pattachitra and Odissi dance, and a close‑knit village life where community gatherings revolve around festivals and agricultural cycles.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Odia; she reads and writes the Odia script fluently thanks to her primary schooling and daily use at home. While she has had no formal schooling in Hindi or English, she picks up basic Hindi from television and radio, allowing her to understand simple conversations. Her exposure to English is limited to occasional exposure via mobile phones and cartoons, so she can recognize common words but cannot hold a fluent dialogue.
skills_and_expertise: She is skilled at preparing traditional Odia meals, handloom weaving, singing devotional folk songs, recounting local legends, performing basic arithmetic for household budgeting, coordinating village festivals, and tending a kitchen garden.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Cooking traditional Odia dishes', 'Handloom weaving', 'Folk singing (bhajans)', 'Storytelling', 'Basic arithmetic', 'Community event coordination', 'Kitchen gardening']
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Odissi music and devotional bhajans, dancing during festivals, sketching temple‑inspired artworks, experimenting with new recipes in the kitchen, collecting seashells on the beach, playing group games with friends, and participating in storytelling circles where she shares folk tales.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Odissi music', 'Dancing during festivals', 'Sketching temple-inspired art', 'Experimenting with new recipes', 'Collecting seashells at the beach', 'Playing group games with friends', 'Participating in storytelling circles']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Sarsati dreams of mastering Odia folk art techniques like Pattachitra and improving her handloom weaving, aiming to open a modest home‑based studio that sells handcrafted paintings and woven textiles to support her family and preserve her cultural heritage. She also wishes to attend government‑run skill‑development workshops to boost her literacy and eventually become a mentor for younger girls in her village.
persona_clinical_summary: A 20‑year‑old female from rural Odisha, never married, with primary education and Odia as her first language. She is a homemaker or retired individual living in Puri district, with lifestyle cues including traditional cooking and community engagement. No formal English or Hindi proficiency, but basic Hindi from media exposure.

Patient Information:
ରୋଗୀ ଜଣକ ଓଡ଼ିଶାର ଗ୍ରାମାଞ୍ଚଳ ପୁରୀ ଜିଲ୍ଲାର ଜଣେ 20 ବର୍ଷର ମହିଳା। ସେ ଅବିବାହିତ ଏବଂ ଜଣେ ଗୃହିଣୀ ଭାବରେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ କରନ୍ତି। ତାଙ୍କର ଶିକ୍ଷା ସ୍ତର ପ୍ରାଥମିକ ଏବଂ ତାଙ୍କର ପ୍ରଥମ ଭାଷା ଓଡ଼ିଆ। ସେ ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି ଗୁରୁତର ଚିନ୍ତା ଏବଂ ଅବସାଦ ଲକ୍ଷଣ ସହିତ ଉପସ୍ଥାପିତ ହୋଇଛନ୍ତି। ତାଙ୍କର ଲକ୍ଷଣଗୁଡ଼ିକ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ସ୍ଥାୟୀ ନିମ୍ନ ମନୋଭାବ, ଦୈନନ୍ଦିନ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକଳାପରେ ଆଗ୍ରହ ହ୍ରାସ ଏବଂ ନିଦ୍ରାହୀନତା ଅନ୍ତର୍ଭୁକ୍ତ। କୌଣସି ମାନସିକ ଲକ୍ଷଣ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ। ରୋଗୀ ଜଣକ ପାରିବାରିକ ସମ୍ପର୍କ ଏବଂ ଆର୍ଥିକ ଚିନ୍ତା ସହିତ ଜଡ଼ିତ ବର୍ଦ୍ଧିତ ଚାପ ବିଷୟରେ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରନ୍ତି।

Clinical Findings:
ପରୀକ୍ଷା ସମୟରେ, ରୋଗୀ ଜଣକ ଚିନ୍ତା ସହିତ ମନୋଶାରୀରିକ ମନ୍ଥରତା ପ୍ରଦର୍ଶନ କରନ୍ତି। କଥାବାର୍ତ୍ତା ଧୀର ଏବଂ କମ୍ ସ୍ୱରରେ ହୋଇଥାଏ। ମନୋଭାବ ଅବସାଦଗ୍ରସ୍ତ ଏବଂ ପ୍ରଭାବ ସୀମିତ। କୌଣସି ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା ଚିନ୍ତା କିମ୍ବା ଆତ୍ମ-କ୍ଷତି ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ। ସାଧାରଣ ଶାରୀରିକ ପରୀକ୍ଷା ଅସାଧାରଣ ଅଟେ।

Treatment Plan:
ରୋଗୀଙ୍କୁ ପର୍ଯ୍ୟବେକ୍ଷଣ ଏବଂ ସ୍ଥିରତା ପାଇଁ ଭର୍ତ୍ତି କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ତାଙ୍କୁ ଦୈନିକ 50 mg ସର୍ଟ୍ରାଲାଇନ୍ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା ଏବଂ କାଉନସେଲିଂ ସେସନ୍ ପାଇଁ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଆଯାଇଥିଲା। ପାଞ୍ଚ ଦିନ ପରେ ତାଙ୍କୁ ଡିସଚାର୍ଜ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା ଏବଂ ଏକ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ଏକ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ ଆପଏଣ୍ଟମେଣ୍ଟ ଧାର୍ଯ୍ୟ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ଚିକିତ୍ସା କରୁଥିବା ମନୋଚିକିତ୍ସକ ହେଉଛନ୍ତି ଡକ୍ଟର ଅଞ୍ଜଳି ମିଶ୍ର।

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER
